zh.lifeSummary: 當Frederick Von Putelendorf, Graf Von Sommersschenburg IV於1085年出生在德國時，他的父親Frederick von Putelendorf III20歲，母親Adelheid von Stade25歲。他和Agnes van Limburg Countess Palatine of Saxony至少有1個女兒。他去世於1125年在德國薩辛奧黑斯萊本丁爾斯泰特 阿姆享年40歲，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇。

nameMeanings.name: Von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: Von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Frederick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Frederic
meanings: From German. See Friedrich and Frederick .

variants.name: Fredrick
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Fredric
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

en.lifeSummary: When Frederick Von Putelendorf, Graf Von Sommersschenburg IV was born in 1085, in Germany, his father, Frederick von Putelendorf III, was 20 and his mother, Adelheid von Stade, was 25. He had at least 1 daughter with Agnes van Limburg Countess Palatine of Saxony. He died in 1125, in Dingelstedt am Huy, Oschersleben, Saxony, Germany, at the age of 40, and was buried in Holy Roman Empire.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Frederick Von Putelendorf, Graf Von Sommersschenburg IV est né en 1085, à Allemagne, son père, Frederick von Putelendorf III, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Adelheid von Stade, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Agnes van Limburg Countess Palatine of Saxony. Il est décédé en 1125, à Dingelstedt am Huy, Oschersleben, Sachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Saint-Empire romain.

de.Dingelstedt am Huy, Oschersleben, Saxony: Dingelstedt am Huy, Oschersleben, Sachsen, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Frederick Von Putelendorf, Graf Von Sommersschenburg IV 1085 in Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Frederick von Putelendorf III 20 und seine Mutter Adelheid von Stade 25. Er hatte mit Agnes van Limburg Countess Palatine of Saxony mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1125 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Dingelstedt am Huy, Oschersleben, Sachsen, Deutschland und wurde in Heiliges Römisches Reich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Frederick Von Putelendorf, Graf Von Sommersschenburg IV è nato il nel 1085 nel Germania, il suo padre, Frederick von Putelendorf III, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Adelheid von Stade, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Agnes van Limburg Countess Palatine of Saxony. Morì nel 1125, nel Dingelstedt am Huy, Oschersleben, Sachsen, Germania, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolto a Heiliges Römisches Reich.

ja.lifeSummary: Frederick Von Putelendorf, Graf Von Sommersschenburg IVが1085年にドイツで生まれた時，父Frederick von Putelendorf IIIは20歳，母Adelheid von Stadeは25歳であった。彼はAgnes van Limburg Countess Palatine of Saxonyとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1125年にドイツサチセンオシェルスレベンディンジェルステト アム ハイで40歳で死去し，ヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Frederick Von Putelendorf, Graf Von Sommersschenburg IV(이)가 1085년 독일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Frederick von Putelendorf III(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelheid von Stade(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Agnes van Limburg Countess Palatine of Saxony(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1125년 독일 작센 오슈르슬레벤 딩스테드트 아 후이에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Frederick Von Putelendorf, Graf Von Sommersschenburg IV nasceu em 1085, em Alemanha, seu pai, Frederick von Putelendorf III, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Adelheid von Stade, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Agnes van Limburg Countess Palatine of Saxony. Ele faleceu em 1125, em Dingelstedt am Huy, Oschersleben, Sachsen, Alemanha, com 40 anos, e foi sepultado em Heiliges Römisches Reich.

ru.Dingelstedt am Huy, Oschersleben, Saxony: дингелстедт ам хуи, ощирслебен, сачсен, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Frederick Von Putelendorf, Graf Von Sommersschenburg IV родился в 1085 г. в местности Германия, его отцу по имени Frederick von Putelendorf III было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelheid von Stade, 25. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Agnes van Limburg Countess Palatine of Saxony. Он умер в 1125 г. в местности дингелстедт ам хуи, ощирслебен, сачсен, Германия в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности хилигесь рoмищесь раич.

Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Agnes van Limburg Countess Palatine of Saxony. Murió en 1125, en Dingelstedt am Huy, Oschersleben, Sachsen, Alemania, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultado en Heiliges Römisches Reich.
